channel.title: Comments for page "What's The Deal?"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "What's The Deal?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i will be completely honest, i read the entire thing pretending there is canned laughter between dialogues. This reads like an actual Seinfeld episode.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, this is the sort of absurd story I need when I get home from school/work. This does exactly what it tries to do, and does it very well.

Easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: WHAT is the DEAL with AIRLINE FOOD? (+1)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Still don't like the SCP, but this tale delivers on exactly what it wants to do in a very entertaining way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I eagerly anticipate the next installment of the Seinfeld Cinematic Universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is the dumbest thing I've read here in a long, long while.

+1

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
This is a trilogy. All three parts are entries in the 144 Hour Jam Contest. God have mercy on my soul.

1. SCP-4844 - Last Man Standing
2. What's The Deal? (you are here)
3. Clash of the Cosmic Comedians

EDIT: It has officially achieved 1st Place for tales on Day 2 of the contest! Thank you everyone who read it, loved it, despised it, or did both simultaneously. I get a lot of people with both.

Show Commentary
Hide Commentary

After writing SCP-4844, I had no intention of participating in this contest beyond the first day.

And then I had a terrible, awful idea.

The rest is history.

Thank you to everyone who reviewed this before it was posted. Special thanks (as before) to A Random Day, who gave me the idea to add the italicized "training" bits. He also suggested the dragon, but that was in jest. I do not deal in jest. I deal only in cold, hard truth.
